title: planchonella rubicunda
intro: Planchonella rubicunda, also known as the Red Sap tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a tropical evergreen tree native to New Caledonia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its sap, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The sap of the Planchonella rubicunda tree is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and combat infections, providing several health benefits. In herbal preparations, the sap is often used to make tonics, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to relieve skin irritations and promote wound healing.
